articles.title: Milan-Turin 3-2, votes and report cards: Pavlovic, beautiful goal and naive penalty. But from 7 like Athekame
articles.summary: Milan-Turin 3-2, the Rossoneri win and put Napoli back at a distance, taking second place. A victory that was needed like bread after Sunday evening's defeat at the Olimpico against Lazio.

articles.title: Hernanes and Milan's 4-3-3: "Allegri must start from here, he must find something new to give fire and energy to this team"
articles.summary: Hernanes analyzes Milan's victory against Torino on DAZN. After a decidedly bad first half, Allegri's team fired up in the second half with the switch to 4-3-3.

articles.title: Milan wins despite VAR and returns to second place: the updated rankings
articles.summary: Despite a rather self-defeating first half and a refereeing class that was absolutely not up to the standard of Serie A, Milan returned to winning ways and overtook Napoli in the standings, regaining second place! With Torino it ends 3-2, Pav decides ...

articles.title: AC Milan 3-2 Torino: Milan’s French midfielders leave San Siro feeling magnifique
articles.summary: AC Milan played out a game of two halves at San Siro on Saturday evening, as they beat Torino 3-2. articles.title: LIVE MN - Milan-Turin (3-2): IT'S OVER! The Devil beats the Bull and regains second place
articles.summary:
90' + 5' | IT'S OVER! Milan returns to victory after the defeat in Rome and regains second place. 

90' + 5' | Santiago Gimenez is booked for stopping Saul Coco's counterattack.

articles.title: MIL-TOR (3-2): last substitution for Allegri, Odogu on
articles.summary: Allegri runs for cover and in injury time returns to five behind: Saelemaekers comes out and the young Odogu comes in, who goes on to play the right-hander.

articles.title: Turin penalty, Allegri snaps at Doveri: "I try to stay calm but you make me lose my desire. I'll stop coaching"
articles.summary: "I try to stay calm but you make me lose my desire. I'll stop training."

articles.title: Gimenez, returning to the field after the long stop: 144 days have passed
articles.summary: In the 77th minute of Milan-Turin, instead of Christian Pulisic who assisted Rabiot's second goal, AC Milan number 7 Santiago Gimenez returned to the field.

articles.title: MIL-TOR (3-2): Vlasic scores from a penalty. Fourneau assigns a strange penalty
articles.summary: Torino scored a penalty with Vlasic, an unstoppable conversion under the crossbar in the 83rd minute.

articles.title: MIL-TOR (3-1): after 144 days Bebote is back. Gimenez returns to the field
articles.summary: After 144 days, it was October 28, 2025, Santiago Gimenez returns to the field! In the 77th minute of Milan-Turin Allegri brought him on in place of Pulisic: the Mexican was out for a long time due to an ankle problem which also required an operation ...

articles.title: MIL-TOR (3-1): Milan double substitution, Nkunku and Ricci on
articles.summary: Double substitution for Milan in the 70th minute, with Allegri bringing on Nkunku and Ricci for Fullkrug and Fofana.

articles.title: As in the first leg, Rabiot scores: it is the first goal at San Siro for the Frenchman
articles.summary: After a subdued first half, in the 54th minute of the second half Milan took the lead again thanks to a goal from Adrien Rabiot on his return from suspension.

articles.title: Watch: Milan’s French midfield duo score in quick succession to make it 3-1
articles.summary: AC Milan took the lead inside the first 10 minutes of the second half, with Adrien Rabiot getting the decisive touch on Christian Pulisic’s dangerous cross before Youssouf Fofana added another. After an underwhelming first half, Milan managed to get back ahead in the game with an early goal after th

articles.summary: Good first match for Giampaolo at the helm of Cremonese: against Parma he won the survival clash with a clear 2-0. Below is the updated ranking.

articles.title: MIL-TOR (3-1): Fofana also scores! With the change of formation it is another Milan
articles.summary: AC MILAN GOAL, AGAIN! FOFANA! Deserved goal from the Frenchman, good at controlling the area and then placing with his right foot. The Rossoneri number 19 was left guiltily alone, Athekame did well to find him between the lines.

articles.title: MIL-TOR (2-1): Rabiot! With 4-3-3 it's a completely different thing!

articles.title: Home solidity and worrying trends: All the key stats ahead of Milan vs. Torino
articles.summary: The pressure is mounting on AC Milan, following the defeat last time out, and they will now need to get back to basics against Torino at San Siro.  The clash will be the 202nd meeting between Milan and Torino, with the Rossoneri only having faced Roma, Juventus and Inter more times in their history.
